description: Reference accessions, canonical linezolid-resistance 23S rRNA positions, and acquired oxazolidinone resistance genes. Positions use E. coli K-12 23S rRNA numbering (clinical-literature convention). Each position carries a verified PMID. Per-species position maps are computed by fetch-references via global pairwise alignment to E. coli 23S. Coordinates verified from NCBI E-utilities feature tables.

linezolid_positions_ecoli_23s.evidence_note: Kloss et al. 1999 mapped this position in archaeal selection (PMID 10556031)
Original mutational mapping (Kloss 1999); reviewed in Long & Vester 2012
Less prevalent; covered in Long & Vester 2012 review
Methylation target of Cfr (Long et al. 2006, PMID 16801432); also direct mutation site
First clinical description in S. aureus (Tsiodras et al. 2001, PMID 11476839). Most prevalent mutation across genera.
linezolid_positions_ecoli_23s.note: Most prevalent 23S linezolid resistance mutation across genera

cfr.summary: Long KS et al. 2006: Cfr methylates A2503 of 23S rRNA conferring PhLOPSa resistance (phenicols, lincosamides, oxazolidinones, pleuromutilins, streptogramin A).

optrA.summary: Wang Y et al. 2015: optrA is a novel ABC-F gene conferring transferable resistance to oxazolidinones and phenicols in Enterococcus.

poxtA.summary: Antonelli A et al. 2018: poxtA confers phenicol-oxazolidinone-tetracycline resistance.

review_references.summary: Long KS, Vester B. 2012. Resistance to linezolid caused by modifications at its binding site on the ribosome. AAC. Comprehensive review of all 23S linezolid resistance mechanisms.

Kloss P et al. 1999. Resistance mutations in 23S rRNA identify the site of action of the protein synthesis inhibitor linezolid in the ribosomal peptidyl transferase center. JMB. Foundational mutational mapping.

ecoli_reference.label: ecoli_K12_23S_rrlB
ecoli_reference.description: Escherichia coli K-12 MG1655 23S rRNA (rrlB) — coordinate master for E. coli-numbering position mapping

Staphylococcus_aureus.description: Staphylococcus aureus NCTC 8325 23S rRNA (Sa23SA, first of 5 copies)
Staphylococcus_aureus.copy_number_note: S. aureus typically encodes 5 rrn operons; reads pile on a single reference copy

Enterococcus_faecalis.description: Enterococcus faecalis V583 23S rRNA (first of 4 copies)
Enterococcus_faecalis.copy_number_note: E. faecalis typically encodes 4 rrn operons

Enterococcus_faecium.description: Enterococcus faecium DO 23S rRNA (first of 6 copies)
Enterococcus_faecium.copy_number_note: E. faecium typically encodes 6 rrn operons

Streptococcus_pneumoniae.description: Streptococcus pneumoniae TIGR4 23S rRNA (first of 4 copies)
Streptococcus_pneumoniae.copy_number_note: S. pneumoniae typically encodes 4 rrn operons
